next_action: step2 (mask) and step3 (fourier) both resolved on masked-recovery metric; step4 arm B (gaussian+mask) done. Fourier is a NET-NEGATIVE ingredient for this task (see gates.step3_fourier_sanity) -- CLAUDE.md build order step4 arms C/D (fourier-containing mixtures) are now de-prioritized for the masked-recovery goal specifically. Next: decide whether to (a) proceed to Phase 2 multi-seed confirmation of isolated mask per summer-2026-plan.md, or (b) run a mask_prob/architecture sweep first, or (c) test whether fourier helps a DIFFERENT task (e.g. denoising/deblurring) before fully killing it -- fourier's negative result here is masked-recovery-specific, not a generation-quality verdict. Human input requested via pi-updates.md draft, not yet sent.

gates.step0_smoke: PASS - all 4 corruption modes run without crash, finite loss, on real IN-1K
gates.step2_mask_sanity: PASS (2026-07-06) - masked-recovery eval, mask_prob=0.5, n=256 held-out IN-1K val images, seed=0 (RNG-matched across arms). mean_masked_mse: gaussian floor=0.24237, mask treatment=0.11313, VAE-roundtrip oracle=0.01488. normalized_gap = (floor-treatment)/(floor-oracle) = 0.568. Mask-trained model recovers masked regions ~2.1x better than gaussian-trained model (57% of the floor-to-oracle headroom closed) -- masking the training start-state teaches the model to use visible context, which pure-Gaussian training never sees. Loss-curve comparison from earlier (mask ~5.2 vs gaussian ~11.2) is NOT part of this verdict -- retracted separately as an easiness artifact of the training target, unrelated to this outcome-metric-based PASS.
gates.step3_fourier_sanity: RESOLVED, NEGATIVE (2026-07-06) - isolated fourier-corruption training (fourier-cutoff=0.25), same eval protocol as step2. mean_masked_mse=0.40612, WORSE than gaussian floor (0.24237). normalized_gap=-0.720 (negative -- fourier-trained model is worse at masked-recovery than a model that never saw structured corruption at all). Mechanism read: fourier low-pass corruption teaches recovery of missing HIGH-FREQUENCY detail from coarse structure, not recovery of a spatially-masked region from surrounding visible pixels -- these are different tasks, and training on one does not transfer to (and may actively hurt, via distribution mismatch) the other. This is specific to the masked-recovery metric; NOT yet evidence that fourier corruption is bad for structured-start-state EqM generally (no FID/energy-ordering check run on this ckpt).
gates.step4_mixture_arm_B: RESOLVED (2026-07-06) - 2-way gaussian+mask mixture (weights 1.0/1.0/0.0), same eval protocol. mean_masked_mse=0.13692, normalized_gap=0.464. Falls between isolated mask (0.568) and 3-arm mixture (0.449) as expected, but notably closer to the 3-arm mixture than to isolated mask -- confirms the 3-arm mixture's underperformance vs isolated mask is NOT primarily fourier-specific drag (arm B still loses ~0.10 gap vs pure mask despite excluding fourier entirely). Read together with step3's negative fourier result: the gap-closing recipe (gaussian:mask:fourier 1:1:1 vs 1:1:0 vs mask-only) suggests BOTH effects are real -- gradient-budget dilution (any mixing with gaussian reduces the pure-mask signal) AND fourier's own negative contribution (arm B > 3-arm mixture, i.e. removing fourier helps). Fourier's negative pull is smaller in magnitude here (only 1/3 weight) than shown by its isolated -0.720 result, consistent with a real but diluted drag.

needs_user_input.prompt: Fourier corruption isolated result is NEGATIVE for masked-recovery (normalized_gap=-0.720, worse than gaussian baseline). See documentation/pi-updates.md 2026-07-06/07 draft for full table + 3-option ask: (a) drop fourier entirely, proceed to Phase 2 (multi-seed mask confirmation) per summer-2026-plan.md, (b) keep fourier but only test it against a different task (e.g. deblurring, not masked-recovery), or (c) one retune at a different fourier-cutoff before concluding, per CLAUDE.md's 1-retune rule. No compute committed pending this decision.
